prompt:
Bump flux's flake.lock to pull the latest klaus from main. The previous CI failure on this PR was caused by a flaky test in klaus (`TestGenIDUniqueness`) which has now been fixed and merged to klaus main. Updating flux's klaus pin will pick up the fix and let flux CI complete.

## What to do

1. Verify you're on the PR #66 branch: `git branch --show-current` should return `agent/20260518-0746-551c`. Pull latest: `git pull --ff-only origin agent/20260518-0746-551c`.
2. Update only the klaus input: `nix flake lock --update-input klaus` (NOT a full `nix flake update` — we only want klaus bumped).
3. Verify the flake.lock change is minimal: `git diff flake.lock` should show only the klaus input updating to a new rev/narHash. If other inputs changed, you ran the wrong command — reset and try again.
4. Sanity-check the build: `nix develop --command true` should complete (this builds klaus to enter the dev shell — proves the new klaus builds cleanly).

## Commit + push

This is the FINAL commit. Do NOT use `[skip ci]`. We WANT CI to run on this state to verify the full chain works.

Commit message: `flake.lock: bump klaus to pick up GenID entropy fix`

`git add flake.lock && git commit -m 'flake.lock: bump klaus to pick up GenID entropy fix' && git push`

## Constraints

- Per flux CLAUDE.md: never reference Claude/AI in commits. Never merge.
- Stay in your assigned worktree.

## What MUST work before stopping

- `git diff origin/main..HEAD --stat` shows the new commit is JUST a flake.lock change
- The push succeeded
- (CI will run after push; you don't need to wait for it)
